headline: Digital Autonomy Challenged: AI Advances, Privacy Erodes
summary: Today's tech news paints a vivid, often unsettling, picture of a world accelerating into a future defined by powerful AI and pervasive digital control. Leading the charge into contentious territory, the Trump administration's sweeping request for federal workers' medical records has ignited a firestorm, laying bare the ever-present tension between state power and individual privacy. This move is compounded by revelations of LinkedIn controversially scanning users' browser extensions, now the subject of two lawsuits, and an Ars Technica piece explicitly detailing how our digital devices are systematically eroding our right to privacy. The incident of a WireGuard VPN developer locked out of their Microsoft account further underscores the precariousness of our digital infrastructure, highlighting how centralized control can hamstring even critical open-source security efforts.
